codeReview.summary: The feature commit closes the nested foreign read-consistency gap by routing read-path normalization through the shared foreign-shape rewrite before stored-record-link decode, and the focused regressions cover save/get plus get_record/list/list_limit agreement while confirming RecordId-only storage at rest.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature metadata and commit evidence exist, but this mission currently lacks a persisted handoff JSON and transcript entry for the completed worker session cac6a3e7-5c2a-440a-a8cb-2aacde6c11c6, so scrutiny had to reconstruct evidence from git log/git show instead of the documented handoff flow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: eatures.json marks milestone-b-nested-foreign-read-consistency completed with completedWorkerSessionId cac6a3e7-5c2a-440a-a8cb-2aacde6c11c6, but C:\Users\admin\.factory\missions\fb36f232-36a4-4e47-b2c9-192ab540f0fc\handoffs has no matching file and worker-transcripts.jsonl has no matching session/feature entry.

summary: Pass. Commit 7e5e36ebabd831395f548b02f98c3a5663135c1c updates the shared read decode seam (core/src/repository/mod.rs:96, core/src/lib.rs:247-295) so nested foreign rows normalize both { id: ... } objects and string-form record links before hydration, and the regressions in core/tests/integration_db.rs:4068-4232 show nested save/get roundtrip plus consistent hydrated values across get, get_record, list, and list_limit while raw storage remains RecordId-based.
